claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm inquiring about my child. Her name is Alex and she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now, to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, but Alex doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to understand your financial situation better.

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or help with accommodation costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes, food, and other expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. To determine how long you can continue receiving Child Benefit in this situation, I need to understand the amount and regularity of your contributions:

**How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep each week or month, and how regularly do you make these contributions?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £30 a week towards Alex's upkeep, and I do this regularly every week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to check the current Child Benefit payment rates to compare against your contributions.

**Just to confirm - has anyone else (such as the person Alex lives with) also claimed or registered Child Benefit for Alex with HMRC?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else has claimed Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that's useful to know. Let me ask one more important question:

**How long has Alex been living with someone else (away from you), and when did you start making these £30 weekly contributions?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been living with her other parent for a while now, and I've been making the £30 weekly contributions consistently throughout that time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules for when a child lives with someone else, you can normally get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after your child goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims. However, it can continue for longer if you make contributions to your child's upkeep that are at least equal to the Child Benefit payment amount.

The current Child Benefit rate is £23.80 per week for the first child. Since you're contributing £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (which exceeds the Child Benefit payment rate), and you've been doing this consistently, you would be able to continue receiving Child Benefit beyond the initial 8 weeks.

However, I should note that you mentioned Alex has been living with her other parent "for a while now." To give you a definitive answer, it's important to confirm: **Has it been more than 8 weeks since Alex started living with her other parent?** This will affect whether your ongoing contributions now allow you to continue receiving Child Benefit or whether you're still within the initial 8-week period.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been much longer than 8 weeks - it's been quite a few months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to make a determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

Although Alex lives with her other parent rather than with you, you're making consistent weekly contributions of £30 towards her upkeep (food, clothes, and other expenses). This amount exceeds the current Child Benefit payment rate of £23.80 per week for the first child.

According to the rules, while you'd normally only get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child moves to live with someone else, your ongoing financial contributions allow you to continue receiving it beyond that period. Since no one else has claimed Child Benefit for Alex, you can continue to receive it **as long as you maintain these regular contributions**.

**Important reminders:**
- Keep making your £30 weekly contributions consistently
- If your circumstances change, you must contact HMRC to let them know
- Only one person can receive Child Benefit for Alex, so make sure the other parent isn't also claiming for her

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you, but you are making consistent weekly contributions of £30 towards her upkeep (food, clothes, and other expenses). According to the Child Benefit rules for when a child lives with someone else, you normally qualify for 8 weeks after the child goes to live elsewhere if nobody else claims. However, since it has been several months (well beyond 8 weeks) and you are contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (£30 per week exceeds the current rate of £23.80 per week for the first child), your contributions allow you to continue receiving Child Benefit beyond the initial 8-week period. No one else has claimed Child Benefit for Alex, so you are eligible to receive it.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you can continue to receive it as long as you maintain these contributions."}
